title: network.degree_distribution request
description: GENERATED from contract/skills/network.degree_distribution.v1.json. The acceptance authority for this skill.

data.description: A connection snapshot plus the node universe it is drawn over, or one degree per node for that same universe. `mode` is the discriminator, and each mode is a CLOSED object: the fields of the other mode are refused rather than ignored, because a countedConnectionCount supplied beside the rows it is supposed to summarise is an assertion Cortexel would then have to choose between trusting and re-deriving.

oneOf.title: connections
oneOf.description: Cortexel counts the degrees itself from the rows.

nodeUniverse.description: The COMPLETE set of nodes under consideration, including every node that has no connection at all. Both endpoints of every counted connection must lie in it. `complete` must be true: this is what makes the zero bin an observation rather than an artifact of which nodes happened to appear in an edge list.

connections.description: Exactly the connections in the selection, every endpoint a member of the node universe. Every multapse is its own row and is never deduplicated. Weights, delays and synapse models may be present; they do not affect a degree and are never used to filter or group one.

observedTargetIds.description: The targets an MPI rank actually owns, declared under `mpi_target_rank_local` with `direction: in`. It is an ownership declaration, not a filter: it removes no row, and it is meaningful only under a rank-local scope.

scope.description: What this snapshot actually saw. A degree distribution is a completeness claim, so the scope decides which directions are even askable.

oneOf.title: node_degrees
oneOf.description: The caller supplies one degree per node and Cortexel verifies coverage instead of observing the rows.

nodeUniverse.description: The COMPLETE set of nodes whose degree is histogrammed. `complete` must be true, and the supplied degrees must cover it exactly once.

nodeDegrees.description: One degree per node, for EVERY node of the declared universe — a node omitted here is not a node of degree zero, it is an unverifiable histogram.

degrees.description: Exact non-negative integer degrees, parallel to nodeIds.

countedConnectionCount.description: The number of connections COUNTED after the autapse policy was applied. It is the conservation target: under count_edges the supplied degrees are asserted to sum to exactly this number, and under count_unique_neighbors not to exceed it.

excludedAutapseCount.description: How many self-connections were removed under `autapsePolicy: exclude` before the degrees were computed. Accepted only in this mode, where Cortexel cannot see the rows; in connections mode it is derived, and a caller-supplied value is refused rather than trusted.

observedTargetIds.description: The targets an MPI rank actually owns, declared under `mpi_target_rank_local` with `direction: in`. It is an ownership declaration, not a filter.

scope.description: What the snapshot these degrees were counted from actually saw. A degree distribution is a completeness claim, so the scope decides which directions are even askable.

selectionId.description: Names the degree-bearing selection so two figures of the same run cannot be confused for one another.

direction.description: The closed discriminator that replaced the two legacy skills. `in` counts connections arriving at a node; `out` counts connections leaving one. Under a target-rank-local scope only `in` is answerable.

countingPolicy.description: count_edges: every connection ENTRY counts, so a multapse of three contributes three. count_unique_neighbors: every distinct counterpart node counts once, however many parallel connections join the pair. The two give different answers and the difference is scientific, not cosmetic.

autapsePolicy.description: include: a self-connection contributes 1 to the node's in-degree and 1 to its out-degree, and under unique-neighbour counting the node is one of its own neighbours. exclude: self-connections are removed before counting and the removed count is reported.

binning.description: How integer degrees are grouped. Bins are inclusive integer ranges, and each mode is a CLOSED object: `per_integer_degree` is the exact form and makes no decision at all, so it carries no width and no start.

oneOf.title: per_integer_degree
oneOf.description: One bin per integer degree in [0, maxObservedDegree]. The exact, decision-free form.

oneOf.title: integer_width
oneOf.description: Consecutive integer degrees merged into inclusive integer ranges anchored at `start`.

width.description: Consecutive integer degrees per bin. A width of 1 is refused because it is `per_integer_degree` under another name, and two encodings of one figure make the derivation record ambiguous.

start.description: The inclusive lower degree of the first bin. Bin j covers the inclusive integer range [start + j*width, start + (j+1)*width - 1].

normalization.description: count: the exact integer number of NODES per bin. probability: that count divided by the COMPLETE declared node universe, zero-degree nodes included. `density` is deliberately not accepted — see science.derivation.

uncertainty.description: Dispersion across REALIZATIONS of the network. Within one realization a bin count is an exact enumeration and has no sampling uncertainty, so a basis of `neurons` or `trials` is not meaningful here; the supported variants are listed in science.uncertaintySupport.
